items.text: Notice: From October 6 to 10, the Blue Line shuttle will be detoured around Carter Hall due to road repairs. Temporary stops will be added at Lawson Library and the Student Center. A $2 fare applies during the detour. Schedules are posted online by the Transit Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, why is the shuttle being detoured?

options.A: Because work is being done on a road
options.B: Because Carter Hall is being painted
options.C: Because the library is being renovated
options.D: Because the route is being permanently closed

questions.explanation: The notice states the detour is due to road repairs, which the option paraphrases as work being done on a road.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about riding the shuttle during the detour?

options.A: It will become free for all students
options.B: It will stop running on weekends
options.C: It will cost riders money to use
options.D: It will no longer reach the Student Center

questions.explanation: Because a $2 fare applies during the detour, riders can infer the shuttle will cost money to use.

items.text: Notice: The north elevator in Bradley Hall will be out of service from March 12 through March 14 for safety inspection. During this period, the south elevator near Room 110 should be used instead. Repairs are scheduled to finish by Friday afternoon. Please contact Facilities with any questions.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what should people do while the north elevator is closed?

options.A: Use the stairs near the main entrance
options.B: Take the south elevator near Room 110
options.C: Report delays to the Transit Office
options.D: Wait for the inspection to be canceled

questions.explanation: The notice directs people to use the south elevator near Room 110 during the closure.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the north elevator after March 14?

options.A: It will be moved to another building
options.B: It will require a fee to operate
options.C: It will stay closed for several weeks
options.D: It will likely be available again

questions.explanation: Since repairs are scheduled to finish by Friday afternoon, the elevator will likely be available again afterward.

items.text: Notice: The water fountains on the third floor of Hale Science Building will be shut off for cleaning from June 2 to June 4. Fountains on the first and second floors remain open during this time. Work begins at 8:00 AM daily. Questions may be directed to the Facilities Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, which fountains will be unavailable?

options.A: The ones in the Facilities Office
options.B: The ones on the first floor
options.C: The ones on the second floor
options.D: The ones on the third floor

questions.explanation: The notice states the third-floor fountains will be shut off for cleaning.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when does the daily work begin?

options.C: At the end of the day

questions.explanation: The notice says work begins at 8:00 AM daily, which the option restates as eight in the morning.

items.text: Join Campus Cleanup Day! Saturday, April 19, 9:00 AM at Memorial Park. Bring gloves and energy. Volunteers receive a free T-shirt and lunch. A $3 donation supports new recycling bins. Sign up at the Student Center desk by April 17. Help keep our campus green!

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what do volunteers get for participating?

options.A: A parking pass and a map
options.B: A gift card and a poster
options.C: A free shirt and a meal
options.D: A backpack and a water bottle

questions.explanation: The flyer states volunteers receive a free T-shirt and lunch, paraphrased as a free shirt and a meal.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, how should people join the event?

options.B: By signing up at the Student Center
options.D: By arriving early at the park gate

questions.explanation: The flyer instructs people to sign up at the Student Center desk by April 17.
